title: aeluropus littoralis subsp. pungens
intro: Aeluropus littoralis subsp. pungens, also known as Sea Fennel or Beach Grass, is a plant species native to the Mediterranean region, characterized by its upright growth habit and feathery foliage. Traditionally, the plant has been used for its medicinal properties, including its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help to soothe skin irritations, wounds, and respiratory issues. The health benefits of Aeluropus littoralis subsp. pungens include its ability to reduce fever, alleviate digestive issues, and act as a natural diuretic, making it a popular herbal remedy in traditional medicine. Herbal preparations of Aeluropus littoralis subsp. pungens can be made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, which can be consumed orally or applied topically to harness its medicinal properties.
